# AGENTS.md

## How to invoke agents

### VS Code (GitHub Copilot Chat)
Type `@<agentname>` in Copilot Chat — e.g. `@skill-author add a new KEDA command`.
Or open the agent picker dropdown and select from the list.

### Claude Code
Claude Code reads `CLAUDE.md` automatically at session start. The `## Agent Context` section in `CLAUDE.md` summarises the roster and handoffs. Read `AGENTS.md` for full details.

### Codex
Agents are registered in `agents/openai.yaml`. Invoke with `$platform-skills` prefix in a Codex session.

---

## Repo

platform-skills — a production-grade platform engineering handbook delivered as a Claude/Copilot/Cursor/Codex skill. Used by platform engineers to get interactive guidance with blast radius, validation steps, and rollback plans built in.

## Stack

- Content: Markdown (`references/`, `examples/`, `commands/`, `SKILL.md`)
- CI: GitHub Actions (`validate.yml`, `release.yml`)
- Tests: Shell scripts (`tests/handbook-consistency.sh`, `tests/validate-skill.sh`, `tests/release-consistency.sh`)
- Delivery: Claude Code plugin marketplace, Codex skill, Cursor rules, Copilot chat instructions

## How we work

A change to this repo means: edit content → run `bash tests/handbook-consistency.sh && bash tests/validate-skill.sh` locally → open a PR → CI validates → merge to main → release agent runs release checklist → release commit updates versions → tag.

No direct pushes to main. Every change through a PR. Release commits are manual — agent proposes, human executes.

## Off-limits

- `.github/workflows/` — CI-only, not touched by content agents
- `marketplace.json` `source.sha` and `version` fields — set during release only
- `INSTALLATION.md` version fields — set during release only
- Direct commits to `main` — always via PR

## Agent roster

| Agent | Owns | Hands off to |
|-------|------|--------------|
| coordinator | routing, planning, AGENTS.md | everyone |
| skill-author | `skills/`, `references/`, `examples/`, `commands/`, `SKILL.md` | release (version bumps), reviewer (pre-merge) |
| release | release checklist, version sync, `marketplace.json` source.sha | skill-author (content fixes before release) |
| reviewer | pre-merge review, stale path checks, Status label checks | skill-author (fixes), release (release PRs) |
| navigator | read-only orientation, structure questions | task agents when action needed |

## Conventions

- Commit messages: `feat(<scope>): description` / `chore(release): v<version>` — see `git log --oneline -5`
- Every example README must have a `Status:` label
- `SKILL.md` at root and `skills/platform-skills/SKILL.md` must stay in sync — `diff` them after editing either
- New command = `commands/<name>.md` + `references/<name>.md` + row in `COMMANDS.md` + entry in `SKILL.md` command table
